claim_status: No-claim owner-decision synchronization control only; this matrix defines future packet inputs and synchronized document updates but does not name an owner, approve a decision, close a gate, grant authority, authorize implementation, or support a production, compatibility, security, accessibility, performance, release, or daily-driver claim.

decision_scopes.owner_role: architecture owner with legal, security, and maintenance review
required_evidence: ADR-0009 evidence registry and decision-review record
source baseline, provenance, license, SBOM, component, runtime, compatibility, and maintenance evidence
direct validator output and retained artifact digests
exception_policy: exception must name an owner and linked risk
exception must expire and define rollback
support and unsupported-behavior language must change with the exception
prohibited_until: no source baseline is approved
no Servo-derived release code is authorized
no compatibility or performance claim is supported

decision_scopes.independent_reviewer_role: independent UI, accessibility, and platform reviewer
required_evidence: accepted native UI ADR and adapter evidence
page-surface, input, accessibility, assistive-technology, fault, and resource evidence
direct validator output and retained fixture or transcript digests
exception_policy: exception must identify affected toolkit or platform surface
exception must preserve accessibility and authority boundaries
exception must expire before release-path approval
prohibited_until: no toolkit is selected for the release path
no accessibility readiness claim is supported
no page-surface or native-shell production claim is supported

required_evidence: pinned compiler, SDK, linker, and environment manifests
independent fresh-host or approved clean-VM replay with retained logs
source-tree, cache, target-directory, failure, and validator evidence
exception_policy: same-host reruns cannot substitute for independent evidence
waiver must identify the unverified host or toolchain dimension
expiry and replay conditions must be recorded
prohibited_until: no fresh-host reproducibility claim is supported
no broad build confidence is promoted
no release toolchain is approved

required_evidence: schema source and generated artifact identity
real transport, authentication, bounds, timeout, cancellation, and stale-epoch negative tests
accepted evidence bundle and retained test output
exception_policy: exception must preserve deny-by-default capability boundaries
unbounded queues or unauthenticated transport cannot be waived silently
expiry must precede production IPC review
prohibited_until: no production IPC readiness is supported
no process-isolation or renderer-security claim is supported
no task acceptance follows from the matrix

required_evidence: packaged expected-deny probes
effective platform policy and compromised-client results
role matrix, failure triage, and retained probe artifacts
exception_policy: policy names cannot substitute for effective enforcement
any unsupported primitive must remain explicit
exception must be risk-linked, time-bounded, and independently reviewed
prohibited_until: no sandbox readiness is supported
no hostile-browsing safety claim is supported
no renderer or agent security claim is supported

required_evidence: sustained-performance policy comparison for scheduler, memory, cache, lifecycle, pressure, energy, thermal, recovery, and isolation-adjusted dimensions
fixed hardware, OS controls, browser pins, and equal-workload manifest
raw runner artifacts, traces, samples, failures, and statistics
claim bundle and direct validator output
exception_policy: hidden tab discarding or unmatched workloads invalidate the comparison
missing failures cannot be omitted from the denominator
public claim exceptions require explicit expiry and rollback
prohibited_until: no Chrome-class result is supported
no extreme-performance claim is supported
no benchmark readiness is promoted from a manifest alone

required_evidence: executable profile, Space, session, snapshot, and migration schemas
fault, rollback, corruption, deletion, private-session, and data-loss tests
privacy review, retained artifacts, and validator output
exception_policy: real-profile behavior cannot be inferred from synthetic fixtures
data-loss risk must be explicit and rollback-defined
migration exceptions require supported-version and expiry updates
prohibited_until: no production profile format is approved
no data-loss safety claim is supported
no sync or credential-storage claim is supported

decision_scopes.independent_reviewer_role: independent update trust and security reviewer
required_evidence: executable package and update lab
signature, rollback, migration, crash-loop, revocation, and key-separation tests
release support policy and retained artifact digests
exception_policy: research keys cannot authorize production updates
rollback and revocation behavior must remain explicit
stable-channel exceptions require expiry and support-boundary updates
prohibited_until: no production updater is approved
no stable distribution claim is supported
no signing authority is granted

decision_scopes.independent_reviewer_role: independent incident, legal, and release reviewer
required_evidence: private intake and tabletop records
emergency patch, backport, signing/update dry-run, disclosure, and cleanup evidence
role, timing, escalation, backup, and retained record review
exception_policy: private-process and disclosure boundaries cannot be bypassed
emergency patch authority must be named and time-bounded
unresolved incidents require explicit closure owner and escalation
prohibited_until: no supported-security claim is supported
no disclosure authority is granted
no incident closure claim is supported

required_evidence: named qualified backup records
CODEOWNERS, protected-path, access, stale-access, and ownerless-path reconciliation
two-person exercise and retained review evidence
exception_policy: primary-only coverage must remain an explicit blocker
backup exceptions require qualified owner, risk, expiry, and remediation
two-person control cannot be inferred from CODEOWNERS text alone
prohibited_until: no backup ownership readiness is supported
no release or signing authority is granted
no broad owner coverage claim is supported

decision_scopes.owner_role: program architecture owner with release authority owner
decision_scopes.independent_reviewer_role: independent closure reviewer not authorizing the work
required_evidence: all prerequisite gate records and task approval evidence
closure packet identity, validator output, and direct artifact digests
explicit closure, hold, or rejection rationale with claim-boundary updates
exception_policy: PB-020 cannot close while a P0 gate is silently unresolved
any held gate requires explicit expiry, rollback, risk, and support-boundary change
closure review cannot self-approve production or release authority
prohibited_until: no all-information-ready-for-building claim is supported
no broad M1 authorization is granted
no production, release, Chrome-class, or daily-driver claim is supported

unsupported: This matrix does not name human owners or reviewers.
This matrix does not select, reject, or hold any decision.
This matrix does not close PB-002 through PB-020.
This matrix does not approve a proposed task or evidence bundle.
This matrix does not grant production, release, signing, legal, or disclosure authority.
This matrix does not establish standards conformance, compatibility, security, accessibility, performance, or production readiness.

PB-006 is intentionally deferred outside these 11 active synchronization scopes; its not-selected reference-platform decision must be revisited before M1 native-shell implementation or supported-platform, compatibility, accessibility, release, or production claims.
A passing validator is not owner review or independent review.
A future exception must update support boundaries and expire; it cannot silently convert a blocker into readiness.
